subject: Project Management
title: HealthCare.gov Launch
description: Learn HealthCare.gov Launch in one minute.

hook.text: On October 1, 2013, HealthCare.gov launched and immediately crashed. Only 6 people enrolled on day one.

buildup.text: The site was built by 55 contractors using waterfall: sequential handoffs between teams that rarely communicated.

discovery.text: End-to-end testing happened just two weeks before launch. The system couldn't handle even a fraction of the expected load.

twist.text: A tech surge team using agile practices rebuilt the site in weeks — what waterfall teams couldn't deliver in years.

climax.text: HealthCare.gov became the poster child for waterfall failure in government — and the catalyst for federal agile adoption.

punchline.text: 55 contractors, zero integration testing. The site worked as well as the process.

quiz.question: Why did HealthCare.gov fail at launch?
options: 55 contractors used waterfall with almost no integration testing before launch
